title: When To Call 911 About High Blood Pressure
license_notes: Use as patient-guidance support for emergency symptoms with very high blood pressure. Avoid implying AHA endorses local discharge wording.
supports: People with blood pressure higher than 180 over 120 plus symptoms such as chest pain, shortness of breath, back pain, weakness, vision change, or difficulty speaking should call emergency services.
Symptomatic severe blood pressure elevation needs emergency evaluation rather than routine discharge instructions.
